tool.name: multiz progressive fold
tool.description: fold per-query pairwise MAFs into one multi-way MAF for a hinge
tool.command:
mkdir -p pairs &&
## Stage each pairwise-MAF collection element under its query strain name ## (element_identifier). Galaxy stores datasets as .dat, so symlink to a real ## .maf path that the fold loop and multiz can consume by name. #for $el in $pairwise_mafs ln -s '$el' 'pairs/${el.element_identifier}.maf' && printf '%s\n' '$el.element_identifier' >> queries.txt && #end for
## Order query strains closest-first by DESCENDING sourmash similarity to the ## hinge (Decision 5: similarity, 1.0 = identical). NOT mash ascending distance. python '$__tool_directory__/multiz_order.py' --compare-csv '$compare_csv' --hinge '$hinge' --queries \$(cat queries.txt) > order.txt &&
## Progressive multiz fold: start from the closest query's pairwise MAF, then ## fold each next-closest query's pairwise MAF onto the running MAF ## (multiz prev.maf next.maf 1 > merged), per impl/10_multiz.sh:100-110. PREV='' && while read -r Q; do NEXT="pairs/\${Q}.maf" && if [ -z "\$PREV" ]; then PREV="\$NEXT" ; else NEW=\$(mktemp ./multiz_tmp_XXXXXX.maf) && multiz "\$PREV" "\$NEXT" 1 > "\$NEW" && case "\$PREV" in pairs/*) : ;; *) rm -f "\$PREV" ;; esac && PREV="\$NEW" ; fi ; done < order.txt &&
cp "\$PREV" '$folded_maf'

param.name: pairwise_mafs
param.label: Per-query pairwise MAFs for this hinge
param.help: A 'list' collection of the hinge's pairwise alignments (hinge vs each query). The element identifier MUST be the query strain name and the hinge must be the reference (top) row in every block.

param.label: sourmash compare.csv similarity matrix
param.help: N+1 row sourmash compare.csv (header of strain labels, then one similarity row per strain). Similarity is 1.0 = identical; fold order sorts DESCENDING (closest first).

param.label: Hinge strain name
param.help: The reference strain of this fold. Must appear as a label in compare.csv.

validator.message: Letters, digits, underscore, hyphen, dot only

tool.help:
**multiz progressive fold** — build one multi-way MAF for a hinge strain.
[multiz](https://github.com/multiz/multiz) (part of TBA) merges *pairwise* MAF block sets that share a common reference. This tool ports the per-hinge progressive fold from the *Pv4* pangenome pipeline (`impl/10_multiz.sh`): given all of a hinge's pairwise alignments (hinge **H** vs each query **Q**), it grows the multi-way alignment one query at a time, threading every new query through what is already aligned onto **H**'s coordinate scaffold.
-----
**Fold order matters.** multiz threads each new strain transitively through the running alignment, so a distant strain folded early loses blocks that fail to thread. The queries are therefore folded **closest-first**, ordered by **descending** sourmash similarity to the hinge read from `compare.csv` (Decision 5: sourmash reports *similarity*, 1.0 = identical — so the order is DESC, the opposite of the mash *distance* convention).
The fold then runs iteratively:
``` multiz prev.maf next.maf 1 > merged.maf ```
starting from the closest query's pairwise MAF and folding the next-closest each step.
-----
**Inputs**
- *Per-query pairwise MAFs* — a `list` collection of the hinge's pairwise alignments. The **element identifier is the query strain name**, and the hinge must be the reference (top) row of every block. - *compare.csv* — the sourmash N×N similarity matrix used to order the fold. - *Hinge strain name* — the reference strain; must be a label in `compare.csv`.
**Output**
- `{hinge}.multiz.maf` — a single multi-way MAF whose blocks carry the hinge plus every query that threaded.
-----
Ported from `Pv4-pangenome/v3/pipeline/impl/10_multiz.sh`. The upstream pairwise AXT→MAF step (`axtToMaf`, IUC `ucsc_axtomaf`) is **not** part of this tool.
